blurb: Ofcom is the UK regulator for communications services and the online safety regulator under the Online Safety Act. Its 2026/27 strategic approach to AI sets out a broadly technology-neutral, outcomes-based stance on enabling safe AI adoption across telecoms, broadcasting and online platforms.

hiring.note: Official Ofcom Workday careers portal; source verified but current vacancies require Workday/browser extraction.

publications.title: Ofcom's strategic approach to AI, 2026/27: Enabling safe and secure AI adoption
publications.summary: Ofcom's updated strategic approach to AI, applying its broadly technology-neutral, outcomes-based regulatory principles to AI adoption across the sectors it regulates. Includes eight case studies (spectrum/online-safety data lake, deepfake mitigation, trust in AI chatbots, telecoms customer-experience impact, AI in broadcasting, AI in telecoms cyber-security, network management) and flags a further findings publication in H2 2026.

publications.title: Statement: Protecting people from illegal harms online
publications.summary: Ofcom's first-edition illegal harms policy statement, codes of practice and risk assessment guidance setting out online services' duties under the Online Safety Act.

news.title: Online Safety Act: Ofcom moves into phased enforcement (illegal-harms and child-safety codes)
news.summary: Ofcom, as Online Safety Act regulator, has moved into phased enforcement — illegal-harms codes (from March 2025) and child-safety codes (from July 2025) — and is preparing for new AI-relevant duties under the Crime and Policing Act and the Schools and Children's Wellbeing Act. Context for the Grok deepfake enforcement debate. Hub page; specific dates vary by code phase.

notes: Regulator/Online Safety Act site. Date 2024-12-16 from the page's og:published_time meta tag. The /online-safety/ index is a hub with no dated items; only the illegal harms statement carried a clear first-party publication date.
